title: PutObject, Metadata, Checksums, and Encryption Defaults · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible single-object upload and integrity evidence record with one identity, byte, checksum, policy, cache, ownership, cancellation, cleanup, or recovery defect; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in single-object upload and integrity evidence record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Own and close input bodies, preserve cancellation, bound bytes, and distinguish request completion from later business publication.
Select supported checksum algorithms, send full-object evidence, validate service response, and retain an application integrity record.
Set and preserve Content-Type, Content-Disposition, Cache-Control, encoding, language, custom metadata, and tags during replacements and copies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Putobject Body Ownership
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go file-delivery team handles case 5367 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove accepted bytes, checksum, metadata, and encryption without using ETag as a universal digest; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Own and close input bodies, preserve cancellation, bound bytes, and distinguish request completion from later business publication.

Inspect the Putobject Body Ownership trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Putobject Body Ownership.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for own and close input bodies, preserve cancellation, bound bytes, and distinguish request completion from later business publication. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Putobject Body Ownership, keep Putobject Body Ownership connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Write Result Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: storage-go-write-result-evidence-1x96bx" that uses case 9745 to prove accepted bytes, checksum, metadata, and encryption without using ETag as a universal digest. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.
